title: Heavy Metal Blood Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
ironment. They're also found in certain medicines, supplements, and foods. Chemicals used in industry and farming may also contain heavy metals, which can end up in the air, soil, and water.
Heavy metals get into your body in different ways. You might breathe them in, eat them, or absorb them through your skin. Because heavy metals are just about everywhere, it's normal to have some in your body. But if too much heavy metal gets into your body, it can cause heavy metal poisoning , which can be deadly.
Heavy metal poisoning can lead to organ damage, changes in your behavior, or difficulties with thinking and memory. The symptoms of heavy metal poisoning depend on the type of metal, how much is in your body, and your age. The highest risk for serious, long-term health problems from heavy metals is during fetal growth and a child's development.
Poisoning from heavy metals can happen slowly over time or suddenly if you are exposed to a large amount of heavy metal all at once.
The most commonly tested heavy metals are:
Lead 
Mercury 
Arsenic 
Cadmium 
Chromium 
Other metals that may be tested include aluminum, beryllium, cobalt, copper, iron, manganese, nickel, platinum, selenium, silicon, silver, and thallium. You may have a heavy metal blood test for one type of metal, or you may have a test for a group of metals, called a "heavy metal panel."
